title: Lazy-Sunday Charred Egyptian Lamb Braise
description:
A moist and flavorful lamb braise infused with the aroma of rosemary and perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon.
This dish is an Ayurvedic-Tamasic friendly option that's easy to make and packed with nutrients.
instructions: Preheat the tava-griddle over medium-high heat.
Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the lamb to a plate and set aside.
Add olive oil to the tava-griddle and saut0 the chopped onion until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and continue cooking for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the rosemary sprigs and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth and lemon juice, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the tava-griddle.
Return the lamb to the tava-griddle and cover with a lid.
Transfer the tava-griddle to the oven and braise the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender.
Let the lamb rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving with labneh cheese and flatbread.
